SessionStart.description: Load previous session context at startup

hooks.command: bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(echo "$INPUT" | node -pe "JSON.parse(require(\"fs\").readFileSync(0,\"utf8\")).tool_input.command || \"\"" 2>/dev/null || echo ""); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE "terraform\s+apply" && ! echo "$CMD" | grep -qE "terraform\s+plan"; then echo "BLOCK: Run /tf-plan-review first. Never apply without reviewing the plan." >&2; exit 2; fi'

PreToolUse.description: Block terraform apply without prior plan review

hooks.command: bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(echo "$INPUT" | node -pe "JSON.parse(require(\"fs\").readFileSync(0,\"utf8\")).tool_input.command || \"\"" 2>/dev/null || echo ""); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE "kubectl\s+(apply|delete).*--context.*(prod|production)"; then echo "BLOCK: Production kubectl operation detected. Switch to a non-prod context or confirm explicitly." >&2; exit 2; fi'

PreToolUse.description: Block kubectl apply/delete targeting production context

hooks.command: bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(echo "$INPUT" | node -pe "JSON.parse(require(\"fs\").readFileSync(0,\"utf8\")).tool_input.command || \"\"" 2>/dev/null || echo ""); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE "git\s+(push|commit).*--no-verify"; then echo "BLOCK: Do not bypass git hooks with --no-verify." >&2; exit 2; fi'

PreToolUse.description: Block git operations that bypass hooks (--no-verify)

hooks.command: bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(echo "$INPUT" | node -pe "JSON.parse(require(\"fs\").readFileSync(0,\"utf8\")).tool_input.command || \"\"" 2>/dev/null || echo ""); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE "(npm|pnpm|yarn|bun)\s+(run\s+)?(dev|start|build|test|serve)" || echo "$CMD" | grep -qE "(pytest|cargo|go\s+test)"; then if [ -z "$TMUX" ] && [ -z "$TERM_PROGRAM" ]; then echo "[Hook] Consider running in tmux for session persistence: tmux new -s dev" >&2; fi; fi'

PreToolUse.description: Reminder to use tmux for long-running dev commands
Pre-commit quality gate: secrets block, console.log warn, conventional commit check
Block writes to linter/formatter config files — fix the code, not the config
Warn on ad-hoc doc filenames (NOTES, TODO, SCRATCH, etc.) outside structured dirs

hooks.command: bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); FILE=$(echo "$INPUT" | node -pe "JSON.parse(require(\"fs\").readFileSync(0,\"utf8\")).tool_input.file_path || \"\"" 2>/dev/null || echo ""); if echo "$FILE" | grep -qE "\.py$"; then if command -v ruff >/dev/null 2>&1; then ruff format "$FILE" 2>/dev/null; ruff check --fix "$FILE" 2>/dev/null; fi; fi'

PostToolUse.description: Auto-format Python files with ruff after edits

hooks.command: bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); FILE=$(echo "$INPUT" | node -pe "JSON.parse(require(\"fs\").readFileSync(0,\"utf8\")).tool_input.file_path || \"\"" 2>/dev/null || echo ""); if echo "$FILE" | grep -qE "\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$"; then if command -v prettier >/dev/null 2>&1; then prettier --write "$FILE" 2>/dev/null; elif command -v biome >/dev/null 2>&1; then biome format --write "$FILE" 2>/dev/null; fi; fi'

PostToolUse.description: Auto-format TypeScript/JavaScript files with prettier or biome after edits

hooks.command: bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); FILE=$(echo "$INPUT" | node -pe "JSON.parse(require(\"fs\").readFileSync(0,\"utf8\")).tool_input.file_path || \"\"" 2>/dev/null || echo ""); if echo "$FILE" | grep -qE "\.(ts|tsx)$"; then echo "[Hook] Run tsc --noEmit to check types" >&2; fi; if echo "$FILE" | grep -qE "\.py$"; then echo "[Hook] Run mypy/pyright to check types" >&2; fi'

PostToolUse.description: Type-check reminder after editing TypeScript or Python files

hooks.command: bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); FILE=$(echo "$INPUT" | node -pe "JSON.parse(require(\"fs\").readFileSync(0,\"utf8\")).tool_input.file_path || \"\"" 2>/dev/null || echo ""); if echo "$FILE" | grep -qE "\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$"; then if grep -q "console\.log" "$FILE" 2>/dev/null; then echo "[Hook] WARNING: console.log found in $FILE — remove before commit" >&2; fi; fi'

PostToolUse.description: Warn about console.log statements in JS/TS files

PreCompact.description: Save compaction marker to active session file before context is summarized

hooks.command: bash -c 'echo "[Session] Remember to commit your work and run tests before ending." >&2'

Stop.description: Session end reminder to commit and test
Persist session summary to ~/.claude/session-data/ after each response
Track token usage and estimated cost to ~/.claude/metrics/costs.jsonl
Signal pattern extraction for sessions with 10+ messages
